samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. The parameter i represents the current bit position of the divisor. The parameter diff represents the difference between the dividend bits and the divisor.

Divide the input numbers A and B into two 8-bit numbers: a_reg and b_reg.

Compare the higher bits of a_reg and b_reg.

If the higher bits of a_reg are greater than or equal to b_reg, subtract b_reg from the higher bits of a_reg and set the quotient bit to 1.

If the higher bits of a_reg are less than b_reg, keep the higher bits of a_reg and set the quotient bit to 0.

Shift the divisor right by one bit and compare again.

Repeat the process until all bits of A are processed.
